key_insight: A time-discrete Cahn-Hilliard step can be reformulated as a four-field auxiliary system that recovers the scalar quantity -Δc through coupled scalar and vector fields, while preserving the usual mass-conserving phase-field gradient-flow structure.
engineering_relevance: This gives a conforming finite element route to solve Cahn-Hilliard problems with improved auxiliary-variable structure, supporting stable simulation of phase separation and related constitutive modeling tasks.
method: Introduce auxiliary fields p and u so that -Δc is represented as 2p + div u.
Derive an equivalent continuous four-field formulation linked to a mixed second-order Stokes/elasticity-type system.
Provide conforming finite element discretization and one-step spatial error estimates for phase-field variables.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports manufactured-solution tests showing expected phase-field convergence, mass conservation, energy decay, and projected consistency of the auxiliary block.
watch_item: The auxiliary block includes an explicit weak-Laplacian recovery defect, so implementation accuracy and element choice may affect the quality of the recovered operator.
paper_title: A four-field auxiliary reformulation of a Cahn-Hilliard time step: Analysis and conforming finite element discretization
